name: Catholic Church
description: The original Christian church tracing its founding to the apostles and Pentecost. Headed by the Bishop of Rome (Pope) with an unbroken line of apostolic succession.

name: Eastern Orthodox Church
description: Split from Rome in the Great Schism of 1054 over papal authority and the Filioque clause. Organized as autocephalous patriarchates.

name: Oriental Orthodox Churches
description: Separated after the Council of Chalcedon (451) over Christological definitions. Includes Coptic, Armenian, Ethiopian, Syriac, and Eritrean churches.

name: Lutheran Church
description: Founded by Martin Luther's Reformation beginning in 1517. Emphasizes justification by faith alone and scripture alone.

name: Anglican / Episcopal
description: Established when Henry VIII broke from papal authority in 1534. Retains much Catholic liturgical tradition while embracing Reformed theology.

name: Reformed / Presbyterian
description: Rooted in John Calvin and Huldrych Zwingli's reforms. Emphasizes God's sovereignty, predestination, and covenant theology.

name: Anabaptist / Mennonite
description: Radical Reformation movement rejecting infant baptism, emphasizing believer's baptism, pacifism, and separation of church and state.

name: Baptist
description: Emerged from English Separatism. Emphasizes believer's baptism by immersion, congregational governance, and individual soul liberty.

name: Methodist
description: Founded by John Wesley's revival movement within Anglicanism. Emphasizes personal holiness, sanctification, and social justice.

name: Pentecostal
description: Emerged from the Holiness movement. Emphasizes baptism in the Holy Spirit, speaking in tongues, and charismatic gifts.
